title: UCB — White Space
html:
UCB White-Space BD Map

Last Updated: May 5, 2026 Purpose: Map every unsold ZoomRx service opportunity across UCB's brand portfolio. Built for BD conversations, AO planning, and pipeline reviews.

How to Read This Matrix

  • Active — currently running or recently delivered
  • HIGH — strong fit, natural extension, client likely receptive
  • MEDIUM — good fit but needs introduction, pilot, or champion building
  • LOW — possible but not a natural fit right now
  • N/A — service does not fit this brand/TA

Immunology: BIMZELX (bimekizumab)

Service Derm (PsO/HS) Rheum (PsA/axSpA)
PET / SFEA Active (Derm PET W1) Active (Rheum PET kick-off)
OCE Tracking MEDIUM — depends on whether UCB deploys dedicated OCE teams for Bimzelx LOW
MSL / MEE HIGH — Bimzelx's multi-indication expansion requires MSL education across derm/rheum; dual IL-17A/F mechanism needs scientific positioning vs. IL-23 class HIGH — rheumatology MSLs critical for PsA/axSpA; scientific differentiation from Cosentyx/Taltz
FRM Tracking MEDIUM — relevant if UCB has dedicated field reimbursement for Bimzelx specialty access MEDIUM
KAM Tracking MEDIUM — relevant for specialty pharmacy and institution-level dynamics LOW
ATU HIGH — HS is a new launch (Nov 2024) with significant awareness gap; ATU baseline needed MEDIUM — PsA/axSpA approvals are Sep 2024, still early enough for awareness tracking
HCP-Patient Conversations Active (completed) — re-fielding opportunity for HS indication MEDIUM — rheumatologist-patient conversations differ from derm
Digital Tracking MEDIUM — Bimzelx online presence vs. Skyrizi/Cosentyx digital share LOW
Patient Experience MEDIUM — oral candidiasis and IBD monitoring concerns create patient experience questions LOW
Qual Research MEDIUM — HS prescribing journey (surgeon vs. derm referral patterns) LOW
Message Testing HIGH — 5 indications in 13 months means multiple concurrent message needs: HS launch, PsA positioning, dual IL-17 mechanism story HIGH — axSpA/PsA competitive messaging vs. Cosentyx/Taltz/Rinvoq
Commercial Analytics / SFTO MEDIUM — Rx integration possible as PET matures LOW
Launch KPI Tracking HIGH — HS launch (Nov 2024) needs KPI framework; Bimzelx still in launch phase across PsA/axSpA MEDIUM
Ferma Congress MEDIUM — AAD, ACR, EULAR congress tracking for Bimzelx competitive intelligence MEDIUM
Social Listening MEDIUM — patient online conversations about Bimzelx vs. competitors, AE discussions LOW
Patient Scribe LOW N/A
AI Dynamic Dialogues MEDIUM — applicable for multi-market Bimzelx tracking if UCB goes global with ZoomRx LOW

Neurology — Epilepsy: BRIVIACT, FINTEPLA, NAYZILAM

Service FINTEPLA (Dravet/LGS) BRIVIACT (Focal Seizures) NAYZILAM (Rescue)
PET / SFEA Active (Fintepla PET) Active (completed; renewal TBD) LOW — rescue therapy promotion is different from brand PET
OCE Tracking LOW — rare epilepsy HCP universe is small LOW N/A
MSL / MEE HIGH — rare epilepsy MSLs are critical for Dravet/LGS education, REMS navigation, and Rett expansion; MEE would track MSL scientific impact MEDIUM — focal seizure MSLs relevant but less differentiated N/A
FRM Tracking MEDIUM — Fintepla REMS creates access barriers requiring FRM support LOW N/A
KAM Tracking MEDIUM — epilepsy centers and children's hospitals are key accounts LOW N/A
ATU HIGH — Rett syndrome approval would need awareness/trial baseline in new HCP audience (pediatric neurologists) LOW — mature brand Active (Nayzilam+Staccato ATU W1 complete)
HCP-Patient Conversations MEDIUM — caregiver-HCP conversations in Dravet are complex and underexplored LOW LOW
Digital Tracking LOW LOW N/A
Patient Experience MEDIUM — REMS monitoring burden on caregivers (echocardiograms) is a real barrier LOW LOW
Qual Research Active (included in Fintepla PET Qual) Active (Non-User Study completed) LOW
Message Testing MEDIUM — Rett syndrome launch messaging; LGS competitive positioning vs. Epidiolex LOW LOW
Commercial Analytics / SFTO LOW — rare disease market; small Rx volumes LOW N/A
Launch KPI Tracking HIGH — Rett syndrome approval would require launch KPI framework N/A — mature brand N/A
Ferma Congress MEDIUM — AES, AAN congress tracking for epilepsy competitive intelligence LOW N/A
Social Listening MEDIUM — Dravet/LGS caregiver communities are active online LOW LOW
Patient Scribe LOW N/A N/A
AI Dynamic Dialogues LOW LOW N/A

Neurology — gMG: RYSTIGGO, ZILBRYSQ

Service RYSTIGGO (FcRn) ZILBRYSQ (C5)
PET / SFEA HIGH — Rystiggo at +65% YoY growth, actively promoted; Vyvgart competitive dynamic requires tracking MEDIUM — Zilbrysq promotion dynamics differ from Rystiggo; SC C5 vs. IV C5 messaging
OCE Tracking LOW — small HCP universe for gMG LOW
MSL / MEE HIGH — gMG is a specialty market where MSLs drive treatment decisions; mechanism choice (FcRn vs. C5) requires scientific positioning that MSL effectiveness tracking would capture HIGH — same rationale; Zilbrysq's C5 mechanism vs. Soliris/Ultomiris requires MSL differentiation
FRM Tracking MEDIUM — specialty access and reimbursement dynamics for FcRn inhibitors MEDIUM
KAM Tracking MEDIUM — neuromuscular centers of excellence as key accounts MEDIUM
ATU MEDIUM — both brands still building awareness beyond early adopters MEDIUM
HCP-Patient Conversations Active (completed) — re-fielding opportunity as competitive landscape evolves with Vyvgart Hytrulo SC MEDIUM — mechanism-specific conversations
Digital Tracking LOW LOW
Patient Experience MEDIUM — gMG patients have complex treatment journeys; infusion burden, injection site reactions, infection monitoring MEDIUM
Qual Research MEDIUM — mechanism-of-action choice drivers (FcRn vs. C5) are a high-value qual topic MEDIUM
Message Testing HIGH — UCB's dual-mechanism positioning (offering both FcRn and C5) is unique in gMG; message testing for this dual-brand strategy is a natural fit HIGH
Commercial Analytics / SFTO LOW LOW
Launch KPI Tracking MEDIUM — Rystiggo still in growth phase LOW
Ferma Congress MEDIUM — AAN, AANEM congress tracking MEDIUM
Social Listening MEDIUM — MG patient communities are active and vocal online MEDIUM
Patient Scribe LOW LOW
AI Dynamic Dialogues LOW LOW

Top 10 Opportunities (Ranked)

Rank Brand x Service Priority Rationale Entry Point
1 Bimzelx Derm — MSL/MEE HIGH Multi-indication expansion requires scientific positioning; largest brand (€2.2B); MSL effectiveness tracking would capture differentiation vs. IL-23 class Bundle with existing Derm PET relationship
2 Rystiggo — PET HIGH +65% YoY growth, active Vyvgart competition, no ongoing tracking exists. gMG HCP-Pt data provides baseline. Propose as follow-on to completed HCP-Pt study
3 Bimzelx HS — ATU HIGH New launch (Nov 2024), first IL-17 in HS, large awareness gap in surgeon/derm audience Leverage existing Bimzelx derm relationship
4 Fintepla Rett — Launch KPI HIGH Submission Q1 2026; approval triggers launch tracking need for new HCP audience Extension of existing Fintepla PET engagement
5 Bimzelx — Message Testing HIGH 5 indications, 3 HCP audiences, concurrent launch messaging needs Cross-sell from active PET work
6 Rystiggo/Zilbrysq — MSL/MEE HIGH gMG is MSL-driven; mechanism choice (FcRn vs. C5) is a scientific positioning question Propose alongside Rystiggo PET
7 Bimzelx Rheum — MSL/MEE HIGH Rheumatology MSLs critical for PsA/axSpA scientific differentiation Bundle with Rheum PET kick-off
8 Fintepla — MSL/MEE HIGH Rare epilepsy MSLs drive treatment decisions; REMS navigation requires scientific support Extend from Fintepla PET
9 Bimzelx HS — Launch KPI HIGH HS launch needs KPI framework; different HCP audience from PsO/PsA Package with HS ATU
10 Rystiggo/Zilbrysq — Message Testing HIGH Dual-mechanism positioning is unique in gMG — UCB is the only company offering both FcRn and C5; message testing for this strategy is high-value Standalone proposal

Summary

Active cells: 8 (PET x3, HCP-Pt x2, ATU x1, Qual x2) HIGH priority white space: 14 cells across MSL/MEE (6), ATU (2), Message Testing (3), Launch KPI (2), PET (1) Biggest untapped service types: MSL/MEE (0 active, 6 HIGH), Message Testing (0 active, 3 HIGH), Launch KPI (0 active, 2 HIGH)

The MSL/MEE gap is the single largest opportunity. UCB has zero MEE engagement despite operating in 3 TAs where MSLs are critical to treatment decisions (rare epilepsy, gMG, immunology). Closing this gap across even 2 TAs would meaningfully expand the account.

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
